description_long: This object consists of seven cylindrical sticks, resembling dynamite, bundled together with four wide black bands. On the front, a digital timer display is mounted on a green circuit board, with visible electronic components and a countdown reading '1:40'. Several colored wires (red, blue, green, yellow) are attached to the circuit board and run along the surface of the sticks, suggesting a complex wiring system. The ends of the cylinders are visible, showing a starburst pattern typical of dynamite sticks. The overall construction appears to be a makeshift explosive device, with the timer and wiring indicating a timed detonation mechanism. The materials appear to be primarily wood or cardboard for the sticks, plastic for the timer casing, and metal for the electronic components.
description: A bundle of seven dynamite-like sticks strapped together with black bands, featuring a digital timer and exposed colored wires on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital timer, circuit board, and colored wires prominently attached to the bundled sticks.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the circular ends of the sticks and the wiring running along the side, with the timer partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain backs of the sticks and black bands, with no visible timer or wiring details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the stick ends and some wiring visible, but the timer is not in view.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The device features a digital timer and multiple colored wires connected to a circuit board, suggesting detonation is triggered by the timer countdown or possibly by manipulating the wiring.
